summary.text: Historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP. Several fund data points are also provided by Finnhub.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Global Min Vol Factor ETF (ACWV) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI AC World Minimum Volatility index. The fund tracks an index of large- and mid-cap global stocks selected and weighted to create a low volatility portfolio. ACWV was launched on Oct 18, 2011 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: 3 Great ETFs That Invest Globally
data.text: These ETFs are a one-stop shop for global diversification.

data.title: Best Low-Volatility ETFs for When the Market is a Roller Coaster
data.text: It's been a volatile few years for the stock market, and that has investors sniffing out low-volatility ETFs. Just understand their strengths and their limitations.

data.title: Charts Suggest Low-Volatility Equities Are Headed Lower
data.text: Charts of companies that display relatively low levels of volatility are suggesting that prices could be heading lower over the short run.

news.t: Best Low-Volatility ETFs for When the Market is a Roller Coaster
Charts Suggest Low-Volatility Equities Are Headed Lower

holdings.infoBox: ACWV is an equity ETF with a total of 416 individual holdings. The top holdings are Johnson & Johnson stock at 1.55%, Cisco Systems at 1.41%, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company at 1.26%, Duke Energy at 1.23%, and Motorola Solutions at 1.18%.
